revision.text:
'''''Shintō montai''''' (真道問対) was originally written in October 1883 by [[w:en:Honda Chikaatsu|Honda Chikaatsu]] ([[w:ja:本田親徳|本田親徳]]), a 19th-century Japanese writer, in Literary Chinese (hence the lack of Japanese ''[[w:en:Kana|kana]]'' in the text). ''Shintō montai'' (真道問対) can also be found at the end of ''The essence of chinkon kishin'' ([[sn:commons:Category:鎮魂帰神の極意|鎮魂帰神の極意]], ''Chinkon kishin no gokui''), which was published by [[w:en:Shintō Tenkōkyo|Shintō Tenkōkyo]] founder Tomokiyo Yoshisane ([[w:ja:友清歓真|友清歓真]]) in 1920. This version is based on Tomokiyo's 1920 edition. The text consists of 114 questions from [[w:en:Soejima Taneomi|Soejima Taneomi]], along with answers from Honda Chikaatsu, about the nature of the divine, the formation of the spirit and the soul, and so on. Punctuation has been added from the ''Shintō montai'' text in: Suzuki, Shigemichi 鈴木重道 (ed.). 1976. ''Honda Chikaatsu zenshū'' 本田親徳全集. Kawaguchi: Sangabō 山雅房.
==Original text== ;眞道問對
:問者 副島種臣 :對者 本田親德 (001) :問 大地有死生乎。 :對 大地球之爲物無增减。 ::有增减者有死生。
(002) :問 齋有幽顯乎。 :對 有。 ::神武紀曰顯齋是也。 ::有顯齋則有幽齋。 ::可知故顯齋而不幽齋非也 ::幽齋而不顯齋亦非也。
(003) :問 幽齋式可得聞乎。 :對 幽齋無有神殿 ::無有奠幣 ::無有祭文。 ::以靈對靈耳。
(004) :問 天帝無始無終乎。 :對 天帝無始無終也。 ::旣以無始無終之力 ::與無始無終之體 ::造無始無終之萬物。 ::其功亦無始無終也。
(005) :問 天之降民 ::或以爲一祖 ::或以爲各祖如何。 :對 天之降民各種各般也。 ::而天之視民一般也。 ::故體無貴賤 ::而有貴賤者人之所命也。
(006) :問 靈魂有增减乎。 :對 不增不减是眞靈魂。
(007) :問 人魂亦然乎。 :對 爲善則增 ::爲惡則减。
(008) :問 天地大原在道乎。 :對 天地大原實在道。 ::鬼神依道而立。 ::人民依道而活。 ::萬物依道而息。
(009) :問 靈力同一乎。 :對 靈無力 ::力無靈。 ::靈力相應 ::而得爲神爲物也。
(010) :問 靈力相應謂之道乎。 :對 靈力相應 ::而生現体。 ::而靈對靈 ::力對力 ::体對体。 ::得爲眞之眞道。
(011) :問 古人論靈 ::而論不及力體也。 :對 以一力、一靈、一體 ::而立論說者悉偏見也。
(012) :問 何謂全力。 :對 動、靜、凝、解、引、弛、分、合也。
(013) :問 何謂全靈。 :對 勇、親、愛、智也。
(014) :問 何謂全體。 :對 剛、柔、流也。
(015) :問 八力有古稱乎。 :對 動力曰大戶地， ::靜力曰大戶邊， ::凝力曰須比遲根， ::解力曰宇比遲根， ::引力曰活杙， ::弛力曰角杙， ::合力曰面足， ::分力曰惶根。
(016) :問 三体有古稱乎。 :對 流體曰葦芽彦遲， ::剛体曰常立， ::柔体曰豐雲野。
(017) :問 魂力体古名 ::人所命乎 ::神所名乎。 :對 日本各祖所名。
(018) :問 四魂之名若漢名如何。 :對 古書 ::勇魂曰荒魂， ::親魂曰和魂， ::愛魂曰幸魂， ::智魂曰奇魂。 ::義譯也。
(019) :問 大地球動力 ::有別稱乎。 :對 大地球之動力別稱 ::健御名方富神也。 ::自此本力 ::而發萬物之動力。 ::故謂之末力。 ::動力固無靈覺 ::而今有靈覺者 ::勇魂相應也。
(020) :問 大地球引力有別稱乎。 :對 大地球引力之別稱 ::大山昨神也。 ::自此本力 ::而發萬物之引力。 ::故謂之末力。 ::引力固無靈覺 ::而今有靈覺者 ::智魂相應也。
(021) :問 大地球凝力有別稱乎。 :對 大地球凝力之別稱 ::大地主神也。 ::自此本力 ::而發萬物之凝力。 ::故謂之末力。 ::凝力固無靈覺 ::而今有靈覺者 ::愛魂相應也。
(022) :問 大地球合力有別稱乎。 :對 大地球合力之別稱 ::大土神也。 ::自此本力而發萬物之合力。 ::故謂之末力。 ::合力固無靈囊 ::而今有靈覺者 ::親魂相應也。
(023) :問 神祇官所祭生魂者何。 :對 動物之本質也。 ::本質固無靈魂 ::又無力德 ::而今有靈覺 ::又有力德者 ::本靈、本力相應也。
(024) :問 神祇官所祭足魂者何。 :對 植物之本質也。 ::本質固無靈覺 ::又無力德 ::而今有靈覺 ::又有力德者 ::本靈、本力相應也。
(025) :問 神祇官所祭玉留魂者何。 :對 山物之本質也。 ::本質固無靈覺 ::又無力德 ::而今有靈覺 ::又有力德者 ::本靈、本力相應也。
(026) :問 動物有貴賤尊卑如何。 :對 神造萬物 ::其體有貴賤尊卑係 ::力德之過不及。 ::非神所關。
(027) :問 有現人成神之説如何。 :對 幽體可變現体， ::現体不可變幽体。
(028) :問 大地球無死生 ::而萬物有死生如何。 :對 本体無死生 ::而末体有存亡也。 ::末体有存亡 ::而子承孫繼。 ::猶無存亡。 ::故身体髮膚不可不重。 ::神自造萬体之始祖 ::誰不與各祖形体同者， ::知子承孫繼之理則知我体 ::爲父祖之遺体 ::而子孫爲我後身， ::四海同胞 ::神人一系 ::身体髮膚之所以不可不重也。 ::唯体也者有靈 ::而爲用 ::而靈也 ::者自己力德之所取 ::而非父祖所讓也。 ::夫重身体髮膚 ::萬物所同然之本情。 ::雖有本情 ::不有靈性。 ::不有靈性 ::故不知道義。 ::知不道義 ::故不知守死善道。 ::萬物之所以不及人也。
(029) :問 足魂者何。 :對 足魂也者 ::植物之本質也。 ::植物固無靈覺 ::又無力德 ::而今有靈覺、有力德者變他物 ::而生靈覺 ::又生力德也。 ::夫足魂者資養他物 ::而死生榮枯者之總稱。 ::因地靈與地力之變化以成動物 ::而生知覺或成凝体 ::而生一種異狀之力德。 ::俗眼者以爲變異 ::夫資養他物 ::而死生榮枯植物之正則也。 ::其變動物變凝体變則也。
(030) :問 玉留魂者何。 :對 玉留魂者山物之本質也。 ::山物固無靈覺 ::又無力德 ::而今有靈覺 ::又有力德者 ::本靈、本力相應也。 ::体有幽体、現体。
(031) :問 動、植、山與剛、柔、流同乎。 :對 動、植、山固剛、柔、流妙合成形者 ::而俗眼不能見 ::造化前之理 ::故且從俗說 ::而爲植動山之三物。 ::實剛、柔、流其本名也。 ::山配剛 ::植配柔 ::動配流。 ::是特因其質夥者而配之。 ::其實動物中備 ::剛、柔、流三質植物中備 ::剛、柔、流三質山物中備 ::剛、柔、流三質也。 ::幽体又備剛、柔、流。 ::而大虛中所有諸 ::現体皆以幽体造之也。
(032) :問 山物無生機乎。 :對 宇宙間豈有無生機乎。 ::今夫石之產子 ::是有生機也。 ::其與他石抱合 ::是有生機也。 ::神之造物與之 ::以力德所以成物。 ::旣已成物 ::而有無生機者 ::無他理也。
(033) :問 大陽光体乎。 :對 大陽闇体也。 ::大陽有雰圍氣而包羅之 ::猶我大地有雰圍氣也。 ::而由衷攸放之光 ::映徹甚雰圍氣 ::而傳諸我大地界圍氣 ::現見之致如光体也。
(034) :問 其光者何。 :對 上帝之 ::色也。
(035) :問 其温者何。 :對 上帝之 ::温也。
(036) :問 荒魂者何。 :對 荒勇也 ::進也。
(037) :問 奇魂者何。 :對 奇智也 ::巧也。
(038) :問 幸魂者何。 :對 幸愛也 ::益也。
(039) :問 和魂者何。 :對 和親也 ::平也。 ::勇、智、親、愛不易之道， ::進、巧、益、平謂唯其用， ::是以隨時觸事百出千化， ::不可以套而視之。
(040) :問 進者前進之謂乎。 :對 曲、折、斜、直 ::當其可進 ::而進也。
(041) :問 勇之用進而已乎。 :對 果也。
(042) :問 智之用巧而已乎。 :對 察也。
(043) :問 愛之用叁而已乎。 :對 育也。
(044) :問 親之用平而已乎。 :對 交也。
(045) :問 天、地、人同道乎。 :對 同道而曰天 ::道曰地 ::道曰人 ::道者各自有形体之大小輕重 ::故命名不同也。
(046) :問 道可得以四魂制之乎。 :對 道也者 ::勇不能動， ::智不能測， ::愛不能奪， ::親不能掠。
(047) :問 何謂大道。 :對 四魂合道 ::而統之曰大道。
(048) :問 人間交際 ::以一魂對之乎 ::以四魂對之乎。 :對 對君以臣道 ::對父以子道 ::其他準而可知。 ::如四魂因時之與地與位臨機應變 ::而發一談話間一音一句之際 ::亦互出互沒不可究極。 ::故曰道也 ::退而反省宜察 ::此一語愛 ::彼一語親 ::此一語智 ::彼一語勇。 ::而後其中道 ::與不中道可得辨明。 ::此謂之反省之道也。
(049) :問 神與道悠久 ::道與神悠久是乎。 :對 道神不二與字 ::爲人言不經 ::悠久對短言不經。
(050) :問 何以證道。 :對 凡證道者要過去 ::現在未來互相證。 ::證道者以道證道。
(051) :問 道者何。 :對 道者單一無雜。
(052) :問 好者何魂。 :對 好自愛出。
(053) :問 神呼吸乎。 :對 神固呼吸 ::故萬物無不呼吸。
(054) :問 有天帝 ::而有大氣乎 ::有大氣 ::而有天帝乎。 :對 有靈而後有力。 ::有力而後有大氣。 ::汝所呼吸之大氣 ::非大地所呼吸之大氣。 ::大地所呼吸之大氣 ::非大陽所呼吸之大氣。 ::故知天帝所呼吸之大氣 ::非現体所呼吸之大氣。
(055) :問 好勇之好亦自愛出乎。 :對 亦自愛出其必有所愛 ::而好勇也。 ::愛之大過者。
(056) :問 義者何魂。 :對 義者四魂各有之 ::而主裁、制、斷、割。 ::配之於四魂則 ::裁者智也 ::制者親也 ::斷者勇也 ::割者愛也。 ::裁者兼彌縫補綴之意， ::制者兼政令法度之意， ::斷者兼果毅敢爲之意， ::割者兼忘身殉難之意。
(057) :問 改過者義乎直靈乎。 :對 改過者義也。 ::直靈者消過于未萠者。 ::各魂各用 ::而直在其中是直靈也。 ::直靈非時者。
(058) :問 直靈似各魂之良 ::知良能者如何。 :對 直靈各魂 ::至精至微之名 ::取决直靈善善美美也。 ::故異於裁、制、斷、割之意。
(059) :問 國君死社稷大夫死邦疆乎。 :對 行仁履義 ::而死者之謂。 ::暴君汚吏不可 ::有死社稷死邦疆之名。
(060) :問 人能全天禀者 ::蓋鮮而得爲神乎。 :對 善魂者得爲神。 ::眞勇而死勇魂率之。 ::眞智而死智魂率之。 ::眞愛而死愛魂率之。 ::眞親而死親魂率之。 ::故德雖不全必得爲一神。
(061) :問 大國魂旣以爲地道全神 ::而更有四魂者如何。 :對 素有四魂也 ::合之則大國魂神。
(062) :問 爲善之方。 :對 神無晝夜言行善 ::所以成善。
(063) :問 人之大欲似名 ::未知神亦有欲乎。 :對 神之大欲唯善 ::人之欲名亦善。
(064) :問 神必俟人問而敎乎。 :對 因不知初問 ::因知而敎。
(065) :問 人魂爲神自高下勝劣乎。 :對 全德爲上三 ::德爲中二 ::德爲下一 ::德爲最下。 ::一善無可記者 ::在草莽間。
(066) :問 何以證善。 :對 以日地月證之。
(067) :敢問 其意。 :對 自造化之始永遠 ::無疆不敢失其道。
(068) :問 制兼政令法度之意者何。 :對 政正也 ::令理也 ::法公也 ::度同也。
(069) :問 戒懼何系。 :對 系義。
(070) :問 欲何系。 :對 欲自四魂出 ::而與義併立。
(071) :問 願何系。 :對 系於欲。
(072) :問 欲與義併立 ::而義裁、制、斷、割也。 ::然則欲不可無對如何。 :對 名也 ::位也 ::壽也 ::富也。 ::名欲美 ::位欲高 ::壽欲長 ::富欲大。
(073) :問 志何系。 :對 系思。
(074) :問 人祖者 ::神特意造乎。 :對 然。 ::其所以然者 ::賜靈魂， ::是其證。
(075) :問 天帝以何爲魂乎。 :對 勇、智、親、愛。
(076) :問 天帝以何爲力乎。 :對 動、靜、引、弛、凝、解、分、合。
(077) :問 天帝以何爲体乎。 :對 剛、柔、流。
(078) :問 然則天帝旣 ::以勇、智、親、愛爲心。 ::以動、靜、引、弛、凝、解、分、合爲力。 ::以流、柔、剛爲体。 ::以爲神也。 ::未審大小如何。 :對 至大無外 ::至小無內。 ::若無所在 ::若無所不在。
(079) :問 忍耐屬義乎。 :對 屬思。
(080) :問 勉强亦屬思乎。 :對 然。
(081) :問 思者何魂。 :對 勇思舅 ::智思智 ::愛思愛 ::親思親。
(082) :問 忍耐勉强人間之事 ::神不煩思之也 ::而至思一字 ::神亦有之乎。 :對 思人所不思忍耐勉 ::强人所不忍耐勉强。
(083) :問 神何忍。 :對 忍所受於天之善。
(084) :問 喜、怒、哀、樂四魂皆有之乎。 :對 四魂皆有。
(085) :問 嗜欲之依体 ::而發者屬愛身之愛乎。 :對 然。 ::是人祖禀天之善之第一義。
(086) :問 天帝全智全能而爲主幸 ::而造多神者何。 :對 天帝造多神 ::所以全智全能。 ::天帝 ::旣造大陽則付之以靈魂、靈力、靈体。 ::旣造大地則付之以靈魂、靈力、靈体。 ::旣造大陰則付之以靈魂、靈力、靈体。 ::而與大陽 ::與大地 ::與大陰 ::與列星 ::永遠不死。 ::而人類必死。 ::其靈亦爲神。 ::天帝若不造多神 ::其爲何主宰。
(087) :問 天帝旣造人類則 ::其保護亦自爲之乎。 :對 地居 ::其一大陽居 ::其二大陰居 ::其三千萬世 ::而弗違。
(088) :問 人精神必憑語乎。 :對 或憑語 ::或不憑語。
(089) :問 祈禱有益乎。 :對 或有益 ::或無益。
(090) :敢問 智之用。 :對 感也 ::悟也 ::覺也 ::察也。
(091) :敢問 愛之用。 :對 造也 ::生也 ::化也 ::育也。
(092) :敢問 親之用。 :對 修也 ::齋也 ::治也 ::平也。
(093) :敢問 勇之用。 :對 奮也 ::勉也 ::克也 ::進也。
(094) :問 名、位、壽、富之四欲 ::可配諸四魂乎。 :對 名配智 ::位配勇 ::壽配愛 ::富配親。
(095) :問 以靈對靈 ::旣已得聞敎未知對者 ::別自他之辨乎。 :對 或以已靈對已靈。 ::或以已靈對一人靈。 ::或以已靈對一國靈。 ::或以已靈對億萬無數靈。 ::其以体對体 ::以力對力亦復然。
(096) :問 魂有經緯乎。 :對 勇、親爲經 ::智、愛爲緯。
(097) :問 經言之義如何。 :對 一一萬萬確乎不易 ::此之謂經言。
(098) :問 緯言之義如何。 :對 操縱與奪其權有我 ::此之謂緯言。
(099) :問 道有公私乎。 :對 道有大小 ::而事有公私。 ::道上自天子下至庶人 ::所同之名。 ::道立而事行。
(100) :問 秘屬私乎。 :對 秘與私異秘者主顧。
(101) :問 惻隱之心屬何魂。 :對 愛之屬。
(102) :問 辭讓之心屬何魂。 :對 道之屬 ::禽、獸、虫、魚無此心。
(103) :問 羞惡之心屬何魂。 :對 四魂皆有禽獸無所羞。
(104) :問 是非之心屬何魂。 :對 四魂皆有是是非非。
(105) :問 諫爭屬何魂。 :對 四魂皆有禽獸所爭者 ::唯色與食。
(106) :問 嚴屬何魂。 :對 嚴親之屬言其貌 ::有不可犯之威。
(107) :問 然則齋莊中正屬親乎。 :對 親之屬。
(108) :問 忠孝爲道如何。 :對 忠孝人心之固有 ::而未形者 ::無君無父之時 ::此心常 ::有故曰道 ::有君有父 ::而事之道立 ::而事君之事字乃屬親魂 ::道立無疆 ::故無道之曰事系人事。
(109) :問 神異者如何。 :對 見異必敬聞怪必戒。
(110) :問 學屬智而敎屬愛乎。 :對 皆屬四魂。
(111) :問 天神地祇之辨。 :對 在無形曰天神 ::在有形曰地祇。
(112) :問 人魂爲神者 ::屬諸天 ::屬諸地乎。 :對 在零圍氣內者屬諸地。 ::零圍氣外者屬諸天。
(113) :問 靈威如何。 :對 靈威者 ::靈魂動 ::非情者 ::况於有情乎。
(114) :問 大國主也者 ::大地之靈 ::固非人体者 ::旣已聽命。 ::不知其稱神子者 ::何等之誤。 :對 古來所稱神子者 ::大國主靈体力之用而已。 ::大地固無后妃亦何子之有。 :明治十六年十月 :鎭魂歸神之極意終
==English translation== (Google Gemini 2.5 Pro translation; please check for errors and improvements)
'''A Catechism on the True Way''' :''(Shindō Mondō)'' *'''Questioner: Soejima Taneomi''' *'''Responder: Honda Chikaatsu''' ;(001)
*'''Question''': Does the great Earth have life and death?
*'''Answer''': The great Earth-sphere, as a thing, does not increase or decrease. :That which has increase and decrease has life and death.
;(002)
*'''Question''': Do rites [齋, ''sai''] have an esoteric [幽, ''yū''] and exoteric [顯, ''ken''] aspect?
*'''Answer''': They do. :The Chronicle of Emperor Jimmu mentions "exoteric rites." If there are exoteric rites, then there must also be esoteric rites. :This can be known. :Therefore, to perform exoteric but not esoteric rites is wrong; to perform esoteric but not exoteric rites is also wrong.
;(003)
*'''Question''': May I hear of the form of esoteric rites?
*'''Answer''': Esoteric rites have no shrine hall, no offerings of cloth, and no written prayers. :It is spirit communing with Spirit, and nothing more.
;(004)
*'''Question''': Is the Celestial Emperor [天帝, ''Tentei''] without beginning and without end?
*'''Answer''': The Celestial Emperor is without beginning and without end. :With a power that is without beginning and end, and a body that is without beginning and end, He created the ten thousand things, which are also without beginning and end. :His work, too, is without beginning and end.
;(005)
*'''Question''': When Heaven sends down the people, some say it is from one ancestor, while others say from various ancestors. :What is the case?
*'''Answer''': When Heaven sends down the people, they are of various kinds and types. :But Heaven's view of the people is as one. :Therefore, the body has no noble or base status; that which creates such status is what has been decreed by man.
;(006)
*'''Question''': Does the spiritual soul [靈魂, ''reikon''] increase or decrease?
*'''Answer''': That which does not increase and does not decrease is the true spiritual soul.
;(007)
*'''Question''': Is the human soul [人魂, ''jinkon''] also thus?
*'''Answer''': If one does good, it increases. :If one does evil, it decreases.
;(008)
*'''Question''': Does the great origin of Heaven and Earth lie in the Way [道, ''Dō'']?
*'''Answer''': The great origin of Heaven and Earth truly lies in the Way. :The gods and spirits [鬼神, ''kishin''] are established according to the Way. :The people live according to the Way. :The ten thousand things breathe according to the Way.
;(009)
*'''Question''': Are Spirit [靈, ''rei''] and Power [力, ''ryoku''] the same?
*'''Answer''': Spirit is without Power. :Power is without Spirit. :When Spirit and Power correspond with each other, they can become gods or things.
;(010)
*'''Question''': Is the correspondence of Spirit and Power called the Way?
*'''Answer''': When Spirit and Power correspond, the manifest body [現体, ''gentai''] is born. :Then, Spirit corresponds to Spirit, Power corresponds to Power, and Body corresponds to Body. :This becomes the true, True Way.
;(011)
*'''Question''': The ancients discussed Spirit, but their discussion did not extend to Power and Body.
*'''Answer''': To establish a theory based on only one of Power, Spirit, or Body is entirely a partial view.
;(012)
*'''Question''': What is the Complete Power [全力, ''zenryoku'']?
*'''Answer''': Motion, stillness, condensation, dissolution, attraction, repulsion, separation, and union.
;(013)
*'''Question''': What is the Complete Spirit [全靈, ''zenrei'']?
*'''Answer''': Valor [勇], affinity [親], love [愛], and wisdom [智].
;(014)
*'''Question''': What is the Complete Body [全体, ''zentai'']?
*'''Answer''': The hard [剛], the soft [柔], and the fluid [流].
;(015)
*'''Question''': Do the Eight Powers have ancient names?
*'''Answer''': The Motive Power is called Ōhotoji. :The Static Power is called Ōhobe. :The Condensing Power is called Suhijini. :The Dissolving Power is called Uhijini. :The Attractive Power is called Iku-gui. :The Repulsive Power is called Tsunu-gui. :The Uniting Power is called Omodaru. :The Separating Power is called Ayakashikone.
;(016)
*'''Question''': Do the Three Bodies have ancient names?
*'''Answer''': The Fluid Body is called Ashikabi-hikoji. :The Hard Body is called Tokotachi. :The Soft Body is called Toyokumono.
;(017)
*'''Question''': Were the ancient names for Soul, Power, and Body designated by men or named by the gods?
*'''Answer''': They were named by the various ancestors of Japan.
;(018)
*'''Question''': How would the names of the Four Souls be if rendered as Chinese names?
*'''Answer''': According to the ancient books, the Valor Soul is called the Wild Soul [荒魂, ''aramitama'']; the Affinity Soul is called the Harmonious Soul [和魂, ''nigimitama'']; the Love Soul is called the Fortunate Soul [幸魂, ''sachimitama'']; and the Wisdom Soul is called the Wondrous Soul [奇魂, ''kushimitama'']. :This is a translation of their meaning.
;(019)
*'''Question''': Does the Motive Power of the great Earth have another name?
*'''Answer''': Another name for the Motive Power of the great Earth is Takeminakata-tomi-no-kami. :From this fundamental power, the motive power of the ten thousand things arises. :Therefore, this latter is called a derivative power. :Motive Power is inherently without spiritual consciousness, but that it now possesses spiritual consciousness is due to its correspondence with the Valor Soul.
;(020)
*'''Question''': Does the Attractive Power of the great Earth have another name?
*'''Answer''': Another name for the Attractive Power of the great Earth is Ōyamatsumi-no-kami. :From this fundamental power, the attractive power of the ten thousand things arises. :Therefore, this latter is called a derivative power. :Attractive Power is inherently without spiritual consciousness, but that it now possesses spiritual consciousness is due to its correspondence with the Wisdom Soul.
;(021)
*'''Question''': Does the Condensing Power of the great Earth have another name?
*'''Answer''': Another name for the Condensing Power of the great Earth is Ōkuninushi-no-kami. :From this fundamental power, the condensing power of the ten thousand things arises. :Therefore, this latter is called a derivative power. :Condensing Power is inherently without spiritual consciousness, but that it now possesses spiritual consciousness is due to its correspondence with the Love Soul.
;(022)
*'''Question''': Does the Uniting Power of the great Earth have another name?
*'''Answer''': Another name for the Uniting Power of the great Earth is Haniyasuhiko-no-kami. :From this fundamental power, the uniting power of the ten thousand things arises. :Therefore, this latter is called a derivative power. :Uniting Power is inherently without spiritual consciousness, but that it now possesses spiritual consciousness is due to its correspondence with the Affinity Soul.
;(023)
*'''Question''': What is the ''Iku-tama'' [生魂, Life Soul] worshipped by the Council of Deities [神祇官, ''Jingikan'']?
*'''Answer''': It is the fundamental essence of animals. :This essence is inherently without a spiritual soul and without power or virtue. :That it now has spiritual consciousness and has power and virtue is due to its correspondence with the fundamental Spirit and fundamental Power.
;(024)
*'''Question''': What is the ''Taru-tama'' [足魂, Sufficient Soul] worshipped by the Council of Deities?
*'''Answer''': It is the fundamental essence of plants. :This essence is inherently without spiritual consciousness and without power or virtue. :That it now has spiritual consciousness and has power and virtue is due to its correspondence with the fundamental Spirit and fundamental Power.
;(o25)
*'''Question''': What is the ''Tama-tsume-tama'' [玉留魂, Jewel-Retaining Soul] worshipped by the Council of Deities?
*'''Answer''': It is the fundamental essence of minerals ["mountain things"]. :This essence is inherently without spiritual consciousness and without power or virtue. :That it now has spiritual consciousness and has power and virtue is due to its correspondence with the fundamental Spirit and fundamental Power.
;(026)
*'''Question''': How is it that animals have statuses of noble and base, high and low?
*'''Answer''': When God creates the ten thousand things, that their bodies have statuses of noble and base, high and low, is due to an excess or deficiency of power and virtue. :It is not something God is concerned with.
;(027)
*'''Question''': What of the theory that a living person can become a god?
*'''Answer''': An esoteric body [幽体, ''yūtai''] can become a manifest body [現体, ''gentai'']. :A manifest body cannot become an esoteric body.
;(028)
*'''Question''': The great Earth has no life and death, yet the ten thousand things have life and death. :How is this?
*'''Answer''': The fundamental body has no life and death, but the derivative bodies have existence and cessation. :Although derivative bodies have existence and cessation, because children carry on and grandchildren succeed, it is as if there is no cessation. :Therefore, one's body, hair, and skin must be valued.
:God created the first ancestors of all the myriad bodies; who does not share the same physical form as their respective ancestor? If one understands the principle of children carrying on and grandchildren succeeding, then one knows that my body is the bequeathed body of my father and ancestors, and that my children and grandchildren are my future self. :The reason why all within the four seas are brothers, why gods and men are of one lineage, and why one's body, hair, and skin must be valued is this. :However, the body possesses a spirit which it uses, and this spirit is something acquired by one's own power and virtue; it is not something handed down from father and ancestors.
:To value one's body, hair, and skin is the fundamental nature common to all things. :Although they have this fundamental nature, they do not have a spiritual nature [靈性, ''reisei'']. :Because they do not have a spiritual nature, they do not know the Way and righteousness. :Because they do not know the Way and righteousness, they do not know how to hold fast to the good Way until death. :This is why the ten thousand things are inferior to man.
;(029)
*'''Question''': What is the Sufficient Soul (''Taru-tama'')?
*'''Answer''': The Sufficient Soul is the fundamental essence of plants. :Plants are inherently without spiritual consciousness and without power or virtue. :That they now have spiritual consciousness and power or virtue is because they have transformed into other things and thereby gained spiritual consciousness and power or virtue. :The Sufficient Soul is a general term for that which nourishes other things and undergoes life, death, flourishing, and withering. :Through the transformation of earth-spirit and earth-power, they can become animals and gain sentience, or become condensed bodies and gain a kind of unique power and virtue. :The vulgar eye sees this as a strange transformation. :However, to nourish other things and to live, die, flourish, and wither is the proper rule for plants. :To transform into an animal or a condensed body is a deviation from this rule.
;(030)
*'''Question''': What is the Jewel-Retaining Soul (''Tama-tsume-tama'')?
*'''Answer''': The Jewel-Retaining Soul is the fundamental essence of minerals. :Minerals are inherently without spiritual consciousness and without power or virtue. :That they now have spiritual consciousness and power or virtue is due to correspondence with the fundamental Spirit and fundamental Power. :The Body has both an esoteric body and a manifest body.
;(031)
*'''Question''': Are animals, plants, and minerals the same as the Hard, Soft, and Fluid?
*'''Answer''': Animals, plants, and minerals are indeed forms made from the wondrous combination of the Hard, Soft, and Fluid. :However, the vulgar eye cannot see the principles that existed before creation, so for now we follow the common parlance and speak of the three things: plants, animals, and minerals. :In truth, Hard, Soft, and Fluid are their fundamental names. :Minerals are paired with the Hard, plants with the Soft, and animals with the Fluid. :This pairing is merely based on their most abundant quality. :In reality, animals are endowed with all three qualities of Hard, Soft, and Fluid; plants are endowed with all three qualities of Hard, Soft, and Fluid; and minerals are endowed with all three qualities of Hard, Soft, and Fluid. :The esoteric body is also endowed with the Hard, Soft, and Fluid. :All manifest bodies existing in the great void are created from esoteric bodies.
;(032)
*'''Question''': Do minerals have a life-principle?
*'''Answer''': In the entire universe, how could there be anything without a life-principle? That a stone produces a "child" [i.e., crystals] is evidence of a life-principle. :That it embraces and unites with other stones is evidence of a life-principle. :When God creates things, He gives them power and virtue, and thus they become things. :Once they have become things, there is no principle by which they could lack a life-principle.
;(033)
*'''Question''': Is the great Sun a luminous body?
*'''Answer''': The great Sun is a dark body. :The great Sun has an atmosphere that envelops it, just as our great Earth has an atmosphere. :The light that is released from its core shines through that atmosphere and is transmitted to the atmospheric sphere of our great Earth, causing the appearance of a luminous body.
;(034)
*'''Question''': What is that light?
*'''Answer''': It is the color of the Supreme God [上帝, ''Jōtei''].
;(035)
*'''Question''': What is that warmth?
*'''Answer''': It is the warmth of the Supreme God.
;(036)
*'''Question''': What is the Wild Soul (''aramitama'')?
*'''Answer''': ''Ara'' is valor; it is advancement.
;(037)
*'''Question''': What is the Wondrous Soul (''kushimitama'')?
*'''Answer''': ''Kushi'' is wisdom; it is skillfulness.
;(038)
*'''Question''': What is the Fortunate Soul (''sachimitama'')?
*'''Answer''': ''Sachi'' is love; it is benefit.
;(039)
*'''Question''': What is the Harmonious Soul (''nigimitama'')?
*'''Answer''': ''Nigi'' is affinity; it is peace. :Valor, wisdom, love, and affinity are the unchanging Way. :Advancement, skillfulness, benefit, and peace are said to be merely their functions. :For this reason, they respond to time and events, appearing in a hundred ways and a thousand transformations. :One cannot view them with a fixed pattern.
;(040)
*'''Question''': Does "advancement" mean only moving forward?
*'''Answer''': It is to advance—whether by curving, bending, slanting, or going straight—when it is right to advance.
;(041)
*'''Question''': Is the function of Valor merely advancement?
*'''Answer''': It is decisiveness.
;(042)
*'''Question''': Is the function of Wisdom merely skillfulness?
*'''Answer''': It is discernment.
;(043)
*'''Question''': Is the function of Love merely benefit?
*'''Answer''': It is nurturance.
;(044)
*'''Question''': Is the function of Affinity merely peace?
*'''Answer''': It is interaction.
;(045)
*'''Question''': Do Heaven, Earth, and Man share the same Way?
*'''Answer''': It is the same Way, but it is called the Way of Heaven, the Way of Earth, and the Way of Man. :Because their respective forms have differences in size, weight, and importance, their names are different.
;(046)
*'''Question''': Can the Way be controlled by the Four Souls?
*'''Answer''': The Way is such that valor cannot move it, wisdom cannot measure it, love cannot seize it, and affinity cannot steal it.
;(047)
*'''Question''': What is the Great Way [大道, ''Daidō'']?
*'''Answer''': When the Four Souls are united in the Way, the統 (''tō'', unified whole) is called the Great Way.
;(048)
*'''Question''': In human interaction, should one respond with one soul or with the Four Souls?
*'''Answer''': One responds to a ruler with the way of a minister, and to a father with the way of a son. :The rest can be known by this standard. :The Four Souls emerge and recede from each other in response to the time, place, and station, adapting to the circumstances. :Even within a single conversation, in the space of a single sound or phrase, they appear and disappear interchangeably and cannot be fully fathomed. :Therefore, it is said of the Way: one should step back and reflect, and carefully discern, "This phrase was love, that phrase was affinity, this phrase was wisdom, that phrase was valor." Afterwards, one can clarify what within it was in accord with the Way and what was not. :This is called the way of self-reflection.
;(049)
*'''Question''': Is it "God and the Way are eternal" or "The Way and God are eternal"?
*'''Answer''': The Way and God are not two. :The word "and" is an impropriety of human speech. :"Eternal" in contrast to "brief" is an impropriety.
;(050)
*'''Question''': How does one verify the Way?
*'''Answer''': To verify the Way, one must mutually verify the past, present, and future. :One verifies the Way with the Way.
;(051)
*'''Question''': What is the Way?
*'''Answer''': The Way is singular and without mixture.
;(052)
*'''Question''': Liking something belongs to which soul?
*'''Answer''': Liking comes from love.
;(053)
*'''Question''': Does God breathe?
*'''Answer''': God certainly breathes. :Therefore, there is nothing among the ten thousand things that does not breathe.
;(054)
*'''Question''': Is it that the Celestial Emperor exists, and therefore the great atmosphere exists? Or that the great atmosphere exists, and therefore the Celestial Emperor exists?
*'''Answer''': First there is Spirit, and after that there is Power. :First there is Power, and after that there is the great atmosphere. :The great atmosphere that you breathe is not the great atmosphere that the Earth breathes. :The great atmosphere that the Earth breathes is not the great atmosphere that the Sun breathes. :Therefore, know that the great atmosphere that the Celestial Emperor breathes is not the great atmosphere that manifest bodies breathe.
;(055)
*'''Question''': Does the "liking" in "liking valor" also come from love?
*'''Answer''': It also comes from love. :One must surely love something, and for that reason, one likes valor. :It is an excess of love.
;(056)
*'''Question''': Righteousness [義, ''gi''] belongs to which soul?
*'''Answer''': Each of the Four Souls has Righteousness. :It governs judging, regulating, deciding, and dividing. :If we assign them to the Four Souls: judging is Wisdom; regulating is Affinity; deciding is Valor; and dividing is Love. :Judging includes the meaning of mending and patching. :Regulating includes the meaning of government and laws. :Deciding includes the meaning of being resolute and daring. :Dividing includes the meaning of forgetting oneself for a cause.
;(057)
*'''Question''': Is correcting a fault a matter of Righteousness or of the Upright Spirit [直靈, ''naohi'']?
*'''Answer''': Correcting a fault is Righteousness. :The Upright Spirit is what extinguishes a fault before it sprouts. :Each soul has its function, and that which is "upright" within them is the Upright Spirit. :The Upright Spirit is not bound by time or circumstance.
;(058)
*'''Question''': The Upright Spirit seems like the "innate good" of each soul. :What is this innate goodness and innate ability?
*'''Answer''': The Upright Spirit is the name for the most refined and subtle aspect of each soul. :A decision taken from the Upright Spirit is perfectly good and perfectly beautiful. :Therefore, it differs from the meaning of judging, regulating, deciding, and dividing.
;(059)
*'''Question''': Should a ruler die for the altars of state, and a great officer die for the domain?
*'''Answer''': This refers to one who dies while practicing benevolence and treading the path of righteousness. :A tyrannical ruler or a corrupt official cannot claim the name of having died for the altars of state or for the domain.
;(060)
*'''Question''': Those who can perfect their heavenly endowment are few, but can they become gods?
*'''Answer''': A good soul can become a god. :One who dies with true valor is led by the Valor Soul. :One who dies with true wisdom is led by the Wisdom Soul. :One who dies with true love is led by the Love Soul. :One who dies with true affinity is led by the Affinity Soul. :Therefore, even if one's virtue is not perfect, one can surely become a god of one type.
;(061)
*'''Question''': Ōkuninushi is already the complete god of the Earthly Way. :Why are there Four Souls in addition?
*'''Answer''': He has always possessed the Four Souls. :When they are united, he is the god Ōkuninushi.
;(062)
*'''Question''': What is the method for doing good?
*'''Answer''': God has no day or night. :To speak and act for the good is how good is accomplished.
;(063)
*'''Question''': It seems humanity's great desire is for fame. :I do not know if God also has desires.
*'''Answer''': God's great desire is only for goodness. :Humanity's desire for fame is also a good.
;(064)
*'''Question''': Must God wait for a person to ask before He teaches?
*'''Answer''': Because of not knowing, one first asks. :Because of knowing, one then teaches.
;(065)
*'''Question''': When human souls become gods, are there ranks of high and low, superior and inferior?
*'''Answer''': One with complete virtue is of the highest rank. :One with three virtues is of the middle rank. :One with two virtues is of the lower rank. :One with one virtue is of the lowest rank. :One with not a single good deed to their name remains amongst the grass and weeds.
;(066)
*'''Question''': How does one prove goodness?
*'''Answer''': One proves it by the Sun, the Earth, and the Moon.
;(067)
*'''Question''': May I venture to ask the meaning of that?
*'''Answer''': From the beginning of creation, for eternity without limit, they do not dare to lose their Way.
;(068)
*'''Question''': What is the meaning of "regulating includes government and laws"?
*'''Answer''': Government is correctness. :Edicts are principle. :Law is public fairness. :Standards are uniformity.
;(069)
*'''Question''': To what does reverence and caution belong?
*'''Answer''': It belongs to Righteousness.
;(070)
*'''Question''': To what does desire belong?
*'''Answer''': Desire comes from the Four Souls and stands alongside Righteousness.
;(071)
*'''Question''': To what does wishing belong?
*'''Answer''': It belongs to desire.
;(072)
*'''Question''': Desire stands alongside Righteousness, and Righteousness involves judging, regulating, deciding, and dividing. :In that case, what are the objects of desire?
*'''Answer''': Fame, status, longevity, and wealth. :For fame, one desires beauty. :For status, one desires height. :For longevity, one desires length. :For wealth, one desires greatness.
;(073)
*'''Question''': To what does aspiration [志, ''kokorozashi''] belong?
*'''Answer''': It belongs to thought.
;(074)
*'''Question''': Did God create the human ancestor with special intent?
*'''Answer''': Yes. :The reason it is so is that He bestowed the spiritual soul. :This is the proof.
;(075)
*'''Question''': What does the Celestial Emperor take as His soul?
*'''Answer''': Valor, Wisdom, Affinity, and Love.
;(076)
*'''Question''': What does the Celestial Emperor take as His power?
*'''Answer''': Motion, stillness, attraction, repulsion, condensation, dissolution, separation, and union.
;(077)
*'''Question''': What does the Celestial Emperor take as His body?
*'''Answer''': The Hard, the Soft, and the Fluid.
;(078)
*'''Question''': In that case, the Celestial Emperor takes Valor, Wisdom, Affinity, and Love as His heart; Motion, Stillness, etc., as His power; and Fluid, Soft, and Hard as His body, thereby being a God. :I am not sure of His size.
*'''Answer''': He is so great there is nothing outside Him, and so small there is nothing inside Him. :It is as if He is nowhere, yet as if there is nowhere He is not.
;(079)
*'''Question''': Does patience belong to Righteousness?
*'''Answer''': It belongs to thought.
;(080)
*'''Question''': Does diligence also belong to thought?
*'''Answer''': Yes.
;(081)
*'''Question''': Thought belongs to which soul?
*'''Answer''': The thought of Valor is Valor. :The thought of Wisdom is Wisdom. :The thought of Love is Love. :The thought of Affinity is Affinity.
;(082)
*'''Question''': Patience and diligence are human affairs; God should have no trouble with such thoughts. :But does God also possess this thing called "thought"?
*'''Answer''': He thinks what man does not think, is patient where man is not patient, and is diligent where man is not diligent.
;(083)
*'''Question''': What does God endure with patience?
*'''Answer''': He endures the goodness He has received from Heaven.
;(084)
*'''Question''': Do joy, anger, sorrow, and pleasure belong to all Four Souls?
*'''Answer''': All Four Souls possess them.
;(085)
*'''Question''': Do the appetites that arise depending on the body belong to the love of self-love?
*'''Answer''': Yes. :This is the primary meaning of the goodness endowed by Heaven to the human ancestor.
;(086)
*'''Question''': The Celestial Emperor is all-wise and all-powerful, and is the Lord. :Why did He create many gods?
*'''Answer''': The Celestial Emperor created many gods in order to be all-wise and all-powerful. :Having created the great Sun, He endowed it with a spiritual soul, spiritual power, and a spiritual body. :Having created the great Earth, He endowed it with a spiritual soul, spiritual power, and a spiritual body. :Having created the great Moon, He endowed it with a spiritual soul, spiritual power, and a spiritual body. :The great Sun, the great Earth, the great Moon, and the constellations live forever and do not die. :But humankind must die. :Their spirits also become gods. :If the Celestial Emperor did not create many gods, what sort of Lord would He preside over?
;(087)
*'''Question''': Since the Celestial Emperor created humankind, does He also protect them Himself?
*'''Answer''': First is dwelling on Earth, second is dwelling in the Sun, third is dwelling in the Moon. :For ten thousand generations, this is not violated.
;(088)
*'''Question''': Must the human spirit rely on words?
*'''Answer''': Sometimes it relies on words, sometimes it does not.
;(089)
*'''Question''': Is prayer beneficial?
*'''Answer''': Sometimes it is beneficial, sometimes it is not.
;(090)
*'''Question''': I venture to ask about the functions of Wisdom.
*'''Answer''': Feeling, realization, awakening, and discernment.
;(091)
*'''Question''': I venture to ask about the functions of Love.
*'''Answer''': Creating, begetting, transforming, and nurturing.
;(092)
*'''Question''': I venture to ask about the functions of Affinity.
*'''Answer''': Cultivating, purifying, governing, and pacifying.
;(093)
*'''Question''': I venture to ask about the functions of Valor.
*'''Answer''': Striving, exerting, overcoming, and advancing.
;(094)
*'''Question''': Can the four desires for fame, status, longevity, and wealth be matched to the Four Souls?
*'''Answer''': Fame matches with Wisdom. :Status matches with Valor. :Longevity matches with Love. :Wealth matches with Affinity.
;(095)
*'''Question''': Regarding "spirit communing with Spirit," I have heard your teaching. :But I do not know if the communing party distinguishes between self and other.
*'''Answer''': Sometimes, one's own spirit communes with one's own spirit. :Sometimes, one's own spirit communes with the spirit of one other person. :Sometimes, one's own spirit communes with the spirit of a nation. :Sometimes, one's own spirit communes with the spirits of countless millions. :The same is true for body communing with body, and power with power.
;(096)
*'''Question''': Does the soul have a warp and a weft?
*'''Answer''': Valor and Affinity are the warp. :Wisdom and Love are the weft.
;(097)
*'''Question''': What is the meaning of "warp-words"?
*'''Answer''': From one to ten thousand, they are fixed and unchanging. :This is what is meant by "warp-words" [i.e., fundamental principles].
;(098)
*'''Question''': What is the meaning of "weft-words"?
*'''Answer''': To control, manipulate, give, and take—the authority for this rests with me. :This is what is meant by "weft-words" [i.e., adaptive application].
;(099)
*'''Question''': Does the Way have public and private aspects?
*'''Answer''': The Way has large and small aspects, while affairs have public and private aspects. :The Way is a name common to all, from the Son of Heaven down to the common people. :When the Way is established, affairs can be carried out.
;(100)
*'''Question''': Does secrecy belong to the private sphere?
*'''Answer''': Secrecy and the private are different. :Secrecy is a matter of primary concern.
;(101)
*'''Question''': To which soul does the heart of compassion belong?
*'''Answer''': It belongs to Love.
;(102)
*'''Question''': To which soul does the heart of deference and yielding belong?
*'''Answer''': It belongs to the Way. :Birds, beasts, insects, and fish do not have this heart.
;(103)
*'''Question''': To which soul does the heart of shame and dislike of evil belong?
*'''Answer''': All Four Souls possess it. :Beasts and birds feel no shame.
;(104)
*'''Question''': To which soul does the heart of right and wrong belong?
*'''Answer''': All Four Souls have their own right and wrong.
;(105)
*'''Question''': To which soul does admonition and remonstrance belong?
*'''Answer''': All Four Souls possess it. :What beasts and birds contend for is only sex and food.
;(106)
*'''Question''': To which soul does solemnity belong?
*'''Answer''': Solemnity belongs to Affinity. :It speaks of an appearance that has a majesty that cannot be violated.
;(107)
*'''Question''': In that case, do gravity, solemnity, impartiality, and correctness belong to Affinity?
*'''Answer''': They belong to Affinity.
;(108)
*'''Question''': How are loyalty and filial piety considered the Way?
*'''Answer''': Loyalty and filial piety are inherent in the human heart but have not yet taken form. :In a time before rulers and fathers, this heart always existed, hence it is called the Way. :When there is a ruler and a father, the "way of serving" is established. :The "serving" in "serving the ruler" belongs to the Affinity soul. :The Way is established without limit. :Therefore, to speak of the way without the "affair" [of serving] is a matter of human relations.
;(109)
*'''Question''': What of strange divine phenomena?
*'''Answer''': Upon seeing a strange sight, one must be reverent. :Upon hearing of a monstrous thing, one must be cautious.
;(110)
*'''Question''': Does learning belong to Wisdom and teaching to Love?
*'''Answer''': Both belong to all Four Souls.
;(111)
*'''Question''': What is the distinction between Heavenly Gods [天神, ''tenshin''] and Earthly Deities [地祇, ''chigi'']?
*'''Answer''': Those who exist in the formless are called Heavenly Gods. :Those who exist in the formed are called Earthly Deities.
;(112)
*'''Question''': When human souls become gods, do they belong to Heaven or to Earth?
*'''Answer''': Those within the ''zero-sphere'' atmosphere [零圍氣, ''reii-ki''] belong to Earth. :Those outside the ''zero-sphere'' atmosphere belong to Heaven.
;(113)
*'''Question''': What is spiritual authority [靈威, ''rei'i'']?
*'''Answer''': Spiritual authority is such that the spiritual soul moves even things without emotion. :How much more so those with emotion?
;(114)
*'''Question''': Ōkuninushi is the spirit of the great Earth and certainly not a human body. :He has already submitted to the Heavenly Mandate. :I do not understand why it is a mistake to call him a "Child of God" [神子, ''shinko''].
*'''Answer''': What has been called "Child of God" since ancient times is merely the functioning of Ōkuninushi's spirit, power, and body. :The great Earth has no empress or consort, so how could it have a child? *'''Meiji 16 [1883], October.''' *'''The ultimate meaning of pacifying the soul and returning to the divine is finished.'''
==Source== *[https://wikisource.org/wiki/%E7%9C%9E%E9%81%93%E5%95%8F%E5%B0%8D 真道問対] on Wikisource
[[Category:Honda Chikaatsu]] [[en:Shintō Montai]] [[Category:Missing wikidata]][[Category:Pages without translation templates]]

revision.comment: adding dummy category to make categories work
revision.text:
'''''Shintō montai''''' (真道問対) was originally written in October 1883 by [[w:en:Honda Chikaatsu|Honda Chikaatsu]] ([[w:ja:本田親徳|本田親徳]]), a 19th-century Japanese writer, in Literary Chinese (hence the lack of Japanese ''[[w:en:Kana|kana]]'' in the text). ''Shintō montai'' (真道問対) can also be found at the end of ''The essence of chinkon kishin'' ([[sn:commons:Category:鎮魂帰神の極意|鎮魂帰神の極意]], ''Chinkon kishin no gokui''), which was published by [[w:en:Shintō Tenkōkyo|Shintō Tenkōkyo]] founder Tomokiyo Yoshisane ([[w:ja:友清歓真|友清歓真]]) in 1920. This version is based on Tomokiyo's 1920 edition. The text consists of 114 questions from [[w:en:Soejima Taneomi|Soejima Taneomi]], along with answers from Honda Chikaatsu, about the nature of the divine, the formation of the spirit and the soul, and so on. Punctuation has been added from the ''Shintō montai'' text in: Suzuki, Shigemichi 鈴木重道 (ed.). 1976. ''Honda Chikaatsu zenshū'' 本田親徳全集. Kawaguchi: Sangabō 山雅房.
==Original text== ;眞道問對
:問者 副島種臣 :對者 本田親德 (001) :問 大地有死生乎。 :對 大地球之爲物無增减。 ::有增减者有死生。
(002) :問 齋有幽顯乎。 :對 有。 ::神武紀曰顯齋是也。 ::有顯齋則有幽齋。 ::可知故顯齋而不幽齋非也 ::幽齋而不顯齋亦非也。
(003) :問 幽齋式可得聞乎。 :對 幽齋無有神殿 ::無有奠幣 ::無有祭文。 ::以靈對靈耳。
(004) :問 天帝無始無終乎。 :對 天帝無始無終也。 ::旣以無始無終之力 ::與無始無終之體 ::造無始無終之萬物。 ::其功亦無始無終也。
(005) :問 天之降民 ::或以爲一祖 ::或以爲各祖如何。 :對 天之降民各種各般也。 ::而天之視民一般也。 ::故體無貴賤 ::而有貴賤者人之所命也。
(006) :問 靈魂有增减乎。 :對 不增不减是眞靈魂。
(007) :問 人魂亦然乎。 :對 爲善則增 ::爲惡則减。
(008) :問 天地大原在道乎。 :對 天地大原實在道。 ::鬼神依道而立。 ::人民依道而活。 ::萬物依道而息。
(009) :問 靈力同一乎。 :對 靈無力 ::力無靈。 ::靈力相應 ::而得爲神爲物也。
(010) :問 靈力相應謂之道乎。 :對 靈力相應 ::而生現体。 ::而靈對靈 ::力對力 ::体對体。 ::得爲眞之眞道。
(011) :問 古人論靈 ::而論不及力體也。 :對 以一力、一靈、一體 ::而立論說者悉偏見也。
(012) :問 何謂全力。 :對 動、靜、凝、解、引、弛、分、合也。
(013) :問 何謂全靈。 :對 勇、親、愛、智也。
(014) :問 何謂全體。 :對 剛、柔、流也。
(015) :問 八力有古稱乎。 :對 動力曰大戶地， ::靜力曰大戶邊， ::凝力曰須比遲根， ::解力曰宇比遲根， ::引力曰活杙， ::弛力曰角杙， ::合力曰面足， ::分力曰惶根。
(016) :問 三体有古稱乎。 :對 流體曰葦芽彦遲， ::剛体曰常立， ::柔体曰豐雲野。
(017) :問 魂力体古名 ::人所命乎 ::神所名乎。 :對 日本各祖所名。
(018) :問 四魂之名若漢名如何。 :對 古書 ::勇魂曰荒魂， ::親魂曰和魂， ::愛魂曰幸魂， ::智魂曰奇魂。 ::義譯也。
(019) :問 大地球動力 ::有別稱乎。 :對 大地球之動力別稱 ::健御名方富神也。 ::自此本力 ::而發萬物之動力。 ::故謂之末力。 ::動力固無靈覺 ::而今有靈覺者 ::勇魂相應也。
(020) :問 大地球引力有別稱乎。 :對 大地球引力之別稱 ::大山昨神也。 ::自此本力 ::而發萬物之引力。 ::故謂之末力。 ::引力固無靈覺 ::而今有靈覺者 ::智魂相應也。
(021) :問 大地球凝力有別稱乎。 :對 大地球凝力之別稱 ::大地主神也。 ::自此本力 ::而發萬物之凝力。 ::故謂之末力。 ::凝力固無靈覺 ::而今有靈覺者 ::愛魂相應也。
(022) :問 大地球合力有別稱乎。 :對 大地球合力之別稱 ::大土神也。 ::自此本力而發萬物之合力。 ::故謂之末力。 ::合力固無靈囊 ::而今有靈覺者 ::親魂相應也。
(023) :問 神祇官所祭生魂者何。 :對 動物之本質也。 ::本質固無靈魂 ::又無力德 ::而今有靈覺 ::又有力德者 ::本靈、本力相應也。
(024) :問 神祇官所祭足魂者何。 :對 植物之本質也。 ::本質固無靈覺 ::又無力德 ::而今有靈覺 ::又有力德者 ::本靈、本力相應也。
(025) :問 神祇官所祭玉留魂者何。 :對 山物之本質也。 ::本質固無靈覺 ::又無力德 ::而今有靈覺 ::又有力德者 ::本靈、本力相應也。
(026) :問 動物有貴賤尊卑如何。 :對 神造萬物 ::其體有貴賤尊卑係 ::力德之過不及。 ::非神所關。
(027) :問 有現人成神之説如何。 :對 幽體可變現体， ::現体不可變幽体。
(028) :問 大地球無死生 ::而萬物有死生如何。 :對 本体無死生 ::而末体有存亡也。 ::末体有存亡 ::而子承孫繼。 ::猶無存亡。 ::故身体髮膚不可不重。 ::神自造萬体之始祖 ::誰不與各祖形体同者， ::知子承孫繼之理則知我体 ::爲父祖之遺体 ::而子孫爲我後身， ::四海同胞 ::神人一系 ::身体髮膚之所以不可不重也。 ::唯体也者有靈 ::而爲用 ::而靈也 ::者自己力德之所取 ::而非父祖所讓也。 ::夫重身体髮膚 ::萬物所同然之本情。 ::雖有本情 ::不有靈性。 ::不有靈性 ::故不知道義。 ::知不道義 ::故不知守死善道。 ::萬物之所以不及人也。
(029) :問 足魂者何。 :對 足魂也者 ::植物之本質也。 ::植物固無靈覺 ::又無力德 ::而今有靈覺、有力德者變他物 ::而生靈覺 ::又生力德也。 ::夫足魂者資養他物 ::而死生榮枯者之總稱。 ::因地靈與地力之變化以成動物 ::而生知覺或成凝体 ::而生一種異狀之力德。 ::俗眼者以爲變異 ::夫資養他物 ::而死生榮枯植物之正則也。 ::其變動物變凝体變則也。
(030) :問 玉留魂者何。 :對 玉留魂者山物之本質也。 ::山物固無靈覺 ::又無力德 ::而今有靈覺 ::又有力德者 ::本靈、本力相應也。 ::体有幽体、現体。
(031) :問 動、植、山與剛、柔、流同乎。 :對 動、植、山固剛、柔、流妙合成形者 ::而俗眼不能見 ::造化前之理 ::故且從俗說 ::而爲植動山之三物。 ::實剛、柔、流其本名也。 ::山配剛 ::植配柔 ::動配流。 ::是特因其質夥者而配之。 ::其實動物中備 ::剛、柔、流三質植物中備 ::剛、柔、流三質山物中備 ::剛、柔、流三質也。 ::幽体又備剛、柔、流。 ::而大虛中所有諸 ::現体皆以幽体造之也。
(032) :問 山物無生機乎。 :對 宇宙間豈有無生機乎。 ::今夫石之產子 ::是有生機也。 ::其與他石抱合 ::是有生機也。 ::神之造物與之 ::以力德所以成物。 ::旣已成物 ::而有無生機者 ::無他理也。
(033) :問 大陽光体乎。 :對 大陽闇体也。 ::大陽有雰圍氣而包羅之 ::猶我大地有雰圍氣也。 ::而由衷攸放之光 ::映徹甚雰圍氣 ::而傳諸我大地界圍氣 ::現見之致如光体也。
(034) :問 其光者何。 :對 上帝之 ::色也。
(035) :問 其温者何。 :對 上帝之 ::温也。
(036) :問 荒魂者何。 :對 荒勇也 ::進也。
(037) :問 奇魂者何。 :對 奇智也 ::巧也。
(038) :問 幸魂者何。 :對 幸愛也 ::益也。
(039) :問 和魂者何。 :對 和親也 ::平也。 ::勇、智、親、愛不易之道， ::進、巧、益、平謂唯其用， ::是以隨時觸事百出千化， ::不可以套而視之。
(040) :問 進者前進之謂乎。 :對 曲、折、斜、直 ::當其可進 ::而進也。
(041) :問 勇之用進而已乎。 :對 果也。
(042) :問 智之用巧而已乎。 :對 察也。
(043) :問 愛之用叁而已乎。 :對 育也。
(044) :問 親之用平而已乎。 :對 交也。
(045) :問 天、地、人同道乎。 :對 同道而曰天 ::道曰地 ::道曰人 ::道者各自有形体之大小輕重 ::故命名不同也。
(046) :問 道可得以四魂制之乎。 :對 道也者 ::勇不能動， ::智不能測， ::愛不能奪， ::親不能掠。
(047) :問 何謂大道。 :對 四魂合道 ::而統之曰大道。
(048) :問 人間交際 ::以一魂對之乎 ::以四魂對之乎。 :對 對君以臣道 ::對父以子道 ::其他準而可知。 ::如四魂因時之與地與位臨機應變 ::而發一談話間一音一句之際 ::亦互出互沒不可究極。 ::故曰道也 ::退而反省宜察 ::此一語愛 ::彼一語親 ::此一語智 ::彼一語勇。 ::而後其中道 ::與不中道可得辨明。 ::此謂之反省之道也。
(049) :問 神與道悠久 ::道與神悠久是乎。 :對 道神不二與字 ::爲人言不經 ::悠久對短言不經。
(050) :問 何以證道。 :對 凡證道者要過去 ::現在未來互相證。 ::證道者以道證道。
(051) :問 道者何。 :對 道者單一無雜。
(052) :問 好者何魂。 :對 好自愛出。
(053) :問 神呼吸乎。 :對 神固呼吸 ::故萬物無不呼吸。
(054) :問 有天帝 ::而有大氣乎 ::有大氣 ::而有天帝乎。 :對 有靈而後有力。 ::有力而後有大氣。 ::汝所呼吸之大氣 ::非大地所呼吸之大氣。 ::大地所呼吸之大氣 ::非大陽所呼吸之大氣。 ::故知天帝所呼吸之大氣 ::非現体所呼吸之大氣。
(055) :問 好勇之好亦自愛出乎。 :對 亦自愛出其必有所愛 ::而好勇也。 ::愛之大過者。
(056) :問 義者何魂。 :對 義者四魂各有之 ::而主裁、制、斷、割。 ::配之於四魂則 ::裁者智也 ::制者親也 ::斷者勇也 ::割者愛也。 ::裁者兼彌縫補綴之意， ::制者兼政令法度之意， ::斷者兼果毅敢爲之意， ::割者兼忘身殉難之意。
(057) :問 改過者義乎直靈乎。 :對 改過者義也。 ::直靈者消過于未萠者。 ::各魂各用 ::而直在其中是直靈也。 ::直靈非時者。
(058) :問 直靈似各魂之良 ::知良能者如何。 :對 直靈各魂 ::至精至微之名 ::取决直靈善善美美也。 ::故異於裁、制、斷、割之意。
(059) :問 國君死社稷大夫死邦疆乎。 :對 行仁履義 ::而死者之謂。 ::暴君汚吏不可 ::有死社稷死邦疆之名。
(060) :問 人能全天禀者 ::蓋鮮而得爲神乎。 :對 善魂者得爲神。 ::眞勇而死勇魂率之。 ::眞智而死智魂率之。 ::眞愛而死愛魂率之。 ::眞親而死親魂率之。 ::故德雖不全必得爲一神。
(061) :問 大國魂旣以爲地道全神 ::而更有四魂者如何。 :對 素有四魂也 ::合之則大國魂神。
(062) :問 爲善之方。 :對 神無晝夜言行善 ::所以成善。
(063) :問 人之大欲似名 ::未知神亦有欲乎。 :對 神之大欲唯善 ::人之欲名亦善。
(064) :問 神必俟人問而敎乎。 :對 因不知初問 ::因知而敎。
(065) :問 人魂爲神自高下勝劣乎。 :對 全德爲上三 ::德爲中二 ::德爲下一 ::德爲最下。 ::一善無可記者 ::在草莽間。
(066) :問 何以證善。 :對 以日地月證之。
(067) :敢問 其意。 :對 自造化之始永遠 ::無疆不敢失其道。
(068) :問 制兼政令法度之意者何。 :對 政正也 ::令理也 ::法公也 ::度同也。
(069) :問 戒懼何系。 :對 系義。
(070) :問 欲何系。 :對 欲自四魂出 ::而與義併立。
(071) :問 願何系。 :對 系於欲。
(072) :問 欲與義併立 ::而義裁、制、斷、割也。 ::然則欲不可無對如何。 :對 名也 ::位也 ::壽也 ::富也。 ::名欲美 ::位欲高 ::壽欲長 ::富欲大。
(073) :問 志何系。 :對 系思。
(074) :問 人祖者 ::神特意造乎。 :對 然。 ::其所以然者 ::賜靈魂， ::是其證。
(075) :問 天帝以何爲魂乎。 :對 勇、智、親、愛。
(076) :問 天帝以何爲力乎。 :對 動、靜、引、弛、凝、解、分、合。
(077) :問 天帝以何爲体乎。 :對 剛、柔、流。
(078) :問 然則天帝旣 ::以勇、智、親、愛爲心。 ::以動、靜、引、弛、凝、解、分、合爲力。 ::以流、柔、剛爲体。 ::以爲神也。 ::未審大小如何。 :對 至大無外 ::至小無內。 ::若無所在 ::若無所不在。
(079) :問 忍耐屬義乎。 :對 屬思。
(080) :問 勉强亦屬思乎。 :對 然。
(081) :問 思者何魂。 :對 勇思舅 ::智思智 ::愛思愛 ::親思親。
(082) :問 忍耐勉强人間之事 ::神不煩思之也 ::而至思一字 ::神亦有之乎。 :對 思人所不思忍耐勉 ::强人所不忍耐勉强。
(083) :問 神何忍。 :對 忍所受於天之善。
(084) :問 喜、怒、哀、樂四魂皆有之乎。 :對 四魂皆有。
(085) :問 嗜欲之依体 ::而發者屬愛身之愛乎。 :對 然。 ::是人祖禀天之善之第一義。
(086) :問 天帝全智全能而爲主幸 ::而造多神者何。 :對 天帝造多神 ::所以全智全能。 ::天帝 ::旣造大陽則付之以靈魂、靈力、靈体。 ::旣造大地則付之以靈魂、靈力、靈体。 ::旣造大陰則付之以靈魂、靈力、靈体。 ::而與大陽 ::與大地 ::與大陰 ::與列星 ::永遠不死。 ::而人類必死。 ::其靈亦爲神。 ::天帝若不造多神 ::其爲何主宰。
(087) :問 天帝旣造人類則 ::其保護亦自爲之乎。 :對 地居 ::其一大陽居 ::其二大陰居 ::其三千萬世 ::而弗違。
(088) :問 人精神必憑語乎。 :對 或憑語 ::或不憑語。
(089) :問 祈禱有益乎。 :對 或有益 ::或無益。
(090) :敢問 智之用。 :對 感也 ::悟也 ::覺也 ::察也。
(091) :敢問 愛之用。 :對 造也 ::生也 ::化也 ::育也。
(092) :敢問 親之用。 :對 修也 ::齋也 ::治也 ::平也。
(093) :敢問 勇之用。 :對 奮也 ::勉也 ::克也 ::進也。
(094) :問 名、位、壽、富之四欲 ::可配諸四魂乎。 :對 名配智 ::位配勇 ::壽配愛 ::富配親。
(095) :問 以靈對靈 ::旣已得聞敎未知對者 ::別自他之辨乎。 :對 或以已靈對已靈。 ::或以已靈對一人靈。 ::或以已靈對一國靈。 ::或以已靈對億萬無數靈。 ::其以体對体 ::以力對力亦復然。
(096) :問 魂有經緯乎。 :對 勇、親爲經 ::智、愛爲緯。
(097) :問 經言之義如何。 :對 一一萬萬確乎不易 ::此之謂經言。
(098) :問 緯言之義如何。 :對 操縱與奪其權有我 ::此之謂緯言。
(099) :問 道有公私乎。 :對 道有大小 ::而事有公私。 ::道上自天子下至庶人 ::所同之名。 ::道立而事行。
(100) :問 秘屬私乎。 :對 秘與私異秘者主顧。
(101) :問 惻隱之心屬何魂。 :對 愛之屬。
(102) :問 辭讓之心屬何魂。 :對 道之屬 ::禽、獸、虫、魚無此心。
(103) :問 羞惡之心屬何魂。 :對 四魂皆有禽獸無所羞。
(104) :問 是非之心屬何魂。 :對 四魂皆有是是非非。
(105) :問 諫爭屬何魂。 :對 四魂皆有禽獸所爭者 ::唯色與食。
(106) :問 嚴屬何魂。 :對 嚴親之屬言其貌 ::有不可犯之威。
(107) :問 然則齋莊中正屬親乎。 :對 親之屬。
(108) :問 忠孝爲道如何。 :對 忠孝人心之固有 ::而未形者 ::無君無父之時 ::此心常 ::有故曰道 ::有君有父 ::而事之道立 ::而事君之事字乃屬親魂 ::道立無疆 ::故無道之曰事系人事。
(109) :問 神異者如何。 :對 見異必敬聞怪必戒。
(110) :問 學屬智而敎屬愛乎。 :對 皆屬四魂。
(111) :問 天神地祇之辨。 :對 在無形曰天神 ::在有形曰地祇。
(112) :問 人魂爲神者 ::屬諸天 ::屬諸地乎。 :對 在零圍氣內者屬諸地。 ::零圍氣外者屬諸天。
(113) :問 靈威如何。 :對 靈威者 ::靈魂動 ::非情者 ::况於有情乎。
(114) :問 大國主也者 ::大地之靈 ::固非人体者 ::旣已聽命。 ::不知其稱神子者 ::何等之誤。 :對 古來所稱神子者 ::大國主靈体力之用而已。 ::大地固無后妃亦何子之有。 :明治十六年十月 :鎭魂歸神之極意終
==English translation== (Google Gemini 2.5 Pro translation; please check for errors and improvements)
'''A Catechism on the True Way''' :''(Shindō Mondō)'' *'''Questioner: Soejima Taneomi''' *'''Responder: Honda Chikaatsu''' ;(001)
*'''Question''': Does the great Earth have life and death?
*'''Answer''': The great Earth-sphere, as a thing, does not increase or decrease. :That which has increase and decrease has life and death.
;(002)
*'''Question''': Do rites [齋, ''sai''] have an esoteric [幽, ''yū''] and exoteric [顯, ''ken''] aspect?
*'''Answer''': They do. :The Chronicle of Emperor Jimmu mentions "exoteric rites." If there are exoteric rites, then there must also be esoteric rites. :This can be known. :Therefore, to perform exoteric but not esoteric rites is wrong; to perform esoteric but not exoteric rites is also wrong.
;(003)
*'''Question''': May I hear of the form of esoteric rites?
*'''Answer''': Esoteric rites have no shrine hall, no offerings of cloth, and no written prayers. :It is spirit communing with Spirit, and nothing more.
;(004)
*'''Question''': Is the Celestial Emperor [天帝, ''Tentei''] without beginning and without end?
*'''Answer''': The Celestial Emperor is without beginning and without end. :With a power that is without beginning and end, and a body that is without beginning and end, He created the ten thousand things, which are also without beginning and end. :His work, too, is without beginning and end.
;(005)
*'''Question''': When Heaven sends down the people, some say it is from one ancestor, while others say from various ancestors. :What is the case?
*'''Answer''': When Heaven sends down the people, they are of various kinds and types. :But Heaven's view of the people is as one. :Therefore, the body has no noble or base status; that which creates such status is what has been decreed by man.
;(006)
*'''Question''': Does the spiritual soul [靈魂, ''reikon''] increase or decrease?
*'''Answer''': That which does not increase and does not decrease is the true spiritual soul.
;(007)
*'''Question''': Is the human soul [人魂, ''jinkon''] also thus?
*'''Answer''': If one does good, it increases. :If one does evil, it decreases.
;(008)
*'''Question''': Does the great origin of Heaven and Earth lie in the Way [道, ''Dō'']?
*'''Answer''': The great origin of Heaven and Earth truly lies in the Way. :The gods and spirits [鬼神, ''kishin''] are established according to the Way. :The people live according to the Way. :The ten thousand things breathe according to the Way.
;(009)
*'''Question''': Are Spirit [靈, ''rei''] and Power [力, ''ryoku''] the same?
*'''Answer''': Spirit is without Power. :Power is without Spirit. :When Spirit and Power correspond with each other, they can become gods or things.
;(010)
*'''Question''': Is the correspondence of Spirit and Power called the Way?
*'''Answer''': When Spirit and Power correspond, the manifest body [現体, ''gentai''] is born. :Then, Spirit corresponds to Spirit, Power corresponds to Power, and Body corresponds to Body. :This becomes the true, True Way.
;(011)
*'''Question''': The ancients discussed Spirit, but their discussion did not extend to Power and Body.
*'''Answer''': To establish a theory based on only one of Power, Spirit, or Body is entirely a partial view.
;(012)
*'''Question''': What is the Complete Power [全力, ''zenryoku'']?
*'''Answer''': Motion, stillness, condensation, dissolution, attraction, repulsion, separation, and union.
;(013)
*'''Question''': What is the Complete Spirit [全靈, ''zenrei'']?
*'''Answer''': Valor [勇], affinity [親], love [愛], and wisdom [智].
;(014)
*'''Question''': What is the Complete Body [全体, ''zentai'']?
*'''Answer''': The hard [剛], the soft [柔], and the fluid [流].
;(015)
*'''Question''': Do the Eight Powers have ancient names?
*'''Answer''': The Motive Power is called Ōhotoji. :The Static Power is called Ōhobe. :The Condensing Power is called Suhijini. :The Dissolving Power is called Uhijini. :The Attractive Power is called Iku-gui. :The Repulsive Power is called Tsunu-gui. :The Uniting Power is called Omodaru. :The Separating Power is called Ayakashikone.
;(016)
*'''Question''': Do the Three Bodies have ancient names?
*'''Answer''': The Fluid Body is called Ashikabi-hikoji. :The Hard Body is called Tokotachi. :The Soft Body is called Toyokumono.
;(017)
*'''Question''': Were the ancient names for Soul, Power, and Body designated by men or named by the gods?
*'''Answer''': They were named by the various ancestors of Japan.
;(018)
*'''Question''': How would the names of the Four Souls be if rendered as Chinese names?
*'''Answer''': According to the ancient books, the Valor Soul is called the Wild Soul [荒魂, ''aramitama'']; the Affinity Soul is called the Harmonious Soul [和魂, ''nigimitama'']; the Love Soul is called the Fortunate Soul [幸魂, ''sachimitama'']; and the Wisdom Soul is called the Wondrous Soul [奇魂, ''kushimitama'']. :This is a translation of their meaning.
;(019)
*'''Question''': Does the Motive Power of the great Earth have another name?
*'''Answer''': Another name for the Motive Power of the great Earth is Takeminakata-tomi-no-kami. :From this fundamental power, the motive power of the ten thousand things arises. :Therefore, this latter is called a derivative power. :Motive Power is inherently without spiritual consciousness, but that it now possesses spiritual consciousness is due to its correspondence with the Valor Soul.
;(020)
*'''Question''': Does the Attractive Power of the great Earth have another name?
*'''Answer''': Another name for the Attractive Power of the great Earth is Ōyamatsumi-no-kami. :From this fundamental power, the attractive power of the ten thousand things arises. :Therefore, this latter is called a derivative power. :Attractive Power is inherently without spiritual consciousness, but that it now possesses spiritual consciousness is due to its correspondence with the Wisdom Soul.
;(021)
*'''Question''': Does the Condensing Power of the great Earth have another name?
*'''Answer''': Another name for the Condensing Power of the great Earth is Ōkuninushi-no-kami. :From this fundamental power, the condensing power of the ten thousand things arises. :Therefore, this latter is called a derivative power. :Condensing Power is inherently without spiritual consciousness, but that it now possesses spiritual consciousness is due to its correspondence with the Love Soul.
;(022)
*'''Question''': Does the Uniting Power of the great Earth have another name?
*'''Answer''': Another name for the Uniting Power of the great Earth is Haniyasuhiko-no-kami. :From this fundamental power, the uniting power of the ten thousand things arises. :Therefore, this latter is called a derivative power. :Uniting Power is inherently without spiritual consciousness, but that it now possesses spiritual consciousness is due to its correspondence with the Affinity Soul.
;(023)
*'''Question''': What is the ''Iku-tama'' [生魂, Life Soul] worshipped by the Council of Deities [神祇官, ''Jingikan'']?
*'''Answer''': It is the fundamental essence of animals. :This essence is inherently without a spiritual soul and without power or virtue. :That it now has spiritual consciousness and has power and virtue is due to its correspondence with the fundamental Spirit and fundamental Power.
;(024)
*'''Question''': What is the ''Taru-tama'' [足魂, Sufficient Soul] worshipped by the Council of Deities?
*'''Answer''': It is the fundamental essence of plants. :This essence is inherently without spiritual consciousness and without power or virtue. :That it now has spiritual consciousness and has power and virtue is due to its correspondence with the fundamental Spirit and fundamental Power.
;(o25)
*'''Question''': What is the ''Tama-tsume-tama'' [玉留魂, Jewel-Retaining Soul] worshipped by the Council of Deities?
*'''Answer''': It is the fundamental essence of minerals ["mountain things"]. :This essence is inherently without spiritual consciousness and without power or virtue. :That it now has spiritual consciousness and has power and virtue is due to its correspondence with the fundamental Spirit and fundamental Power.
;(026)
*'''Question''': How is it that animals have statuses of noble and base, high and low?
*'''Answer''': When God creates the ten thousand things, that their bodies have statuses of noble and base, high and low, is due to an excess or deficiency of power and virtue. :It is not something God is concerned with.
;(027)
*'''Question''': What of the theory that a living person can become a god?
*'''Answer''': An esoteric body [幽体, ''yūtai''] can become a manifest body [現体, ''gentai'']. :A manifest body cannot become an esoteric body.
;(028)
*'''Question''': The great Earth has no life and death, yet the ten thousand things have life and death. :How is this?
*'''Answer''': The fundamental body has no life and death, but the derivative bodies have existence and cessation. :Although derivative bodies have existence and cessation, because children carry on and grandchildren succeed, it is as if there is no cessation. :Therefore, one's body, hair, and skin must be valued.
:God created the first ancestors of all the myriad bodies; who does not share the same physical form as their respective ancestor? If one understands the principle of children carrying on and grandchildren succeeding, then one knows that my body is the bequeathed body of my father and ancestors, and that my children and grandchildren are my future self. :The reason why all within the four seas are brothers, why gods and men are of one lineage, and why one's body, hair, and skin must be valued is this. :However, the body possesses a spirit which it uses, and this spirit is something acquired by one's own power and virtue; it is not something handed down from father and ancestors.
:To value one's body, hair, and skin is the fundamental nature common to all things. :Although they have this fundamental nature, they do not have a spiritual nature [靈性, ''reisei'']. :Because they do not have a spiritual nature, they do not know the Way and righteousness. :Because they do not know the Way and righteousness, they do not know how to hold fast to the good Way until death. :This is why the ten thousand things are inferior to man.
;(029)
*'''Question''': What is the Sufficient Soul (''Taru-tama'')?
*'''Answer''': The Sufficient Soul is the fundamental essence of plants. :Plants are inherently without spiritual consciousness and without power or virtue. :That they now have spiritual consciousness and power or virtue is because they have transformed into other things and thereby gained spiritual consciousness and power or virtue. :The Sufficient Soul is a general term for that which nourishes other things and undergoes life, death, flourishing, and withering. :Through the transformation of earth-spirit and earth-power, they can become animals and gain sentience, or become condensed bodies and gain a kind of unique power and virtue. :The vulgar eye sees this as a strange transformation. :However, to nourish other things and to live, die, flourish, and wither is the proper rule for plants. :To transform into an animal or a condensed body is a deviation from this rule.
;(030)
*'''Question''': What is the Jewel-Retaining Soul (''Tama-tsume-tama'')?
*'''Answer''': The Jewel-Retaining Soul is the fundamental essence of minerals. :Minerals are inherently without spiritual consciousness and without power or virtue. :That they now have spiritual consciousness and power or virtue is due to correspondence with the fundamental Spirit and fundamental Power. :The Body has both an esoteric body and a manifest body.
;(031)
*'''Question''': Are animals, plants, and minerals the same as the Hard, Soft, and Fluid?
*'''Answer''': Animals, plants, and minerals are indeed forms made from the wondrous combination of the Hard, Soft, and Fluid. :However, the vulgar eye cannot see the principles that existed before creation, so for now we follow the common parlance and speak of the three things: plants, animals, and minerals. :In truth, Hard, Soft, and Fluid are their fundamental names. :Minerals are paired with the Hard, plants with the Soft, and animals with the Fluid. :This pairing is merely based on their most abundant quality. :In reality, animals are endowed with all three qualities of Hard, Soft, and Fluid; plants are endowed with all three qualities of Hard, Soft, and Fluid; and minerals are endowed with all three qualities of Hard, Soft, and Fluid. :The esoteric body is also endowed with the Hard, Soft, and Fluid. :All manifest bodies existing in the great void are created from esoteric bodies.
;(032)
*'''Question''': Do minerals have a life-principle?
*'''Answer''': In the entire universe, how could there be anything without a life-principle? That a stone produces a "child" [i.e., crystals] is evidence of a life-principle. :That it embraces and unites with other stones is evidence of a life-principle. :When God creates things, He gives them power and virtue, and thus they become things. :Once they have become things, there is no principle by which they could lack a life-principle.
;(033)
*'''Question''': Is the great Sun a luminous body?
*'''Answer''': The great Sun is a dark body. :The great Sun has an atmosphere that envelops it, just as our great Earth has an atmosphere. :The light that is released from its core shines through that atmosphere and is transmitted to the atmospheric sphere of our great Earth, causing the appearance of a luminous body.
;(034)
*'''Question''': What is that light?
*'''Answer''': It is the color of the Supreme God [上帝, ''Jōtei''].
;(035)
*'''Question''': What is that warmth?
*'''Answer''': It is the warmth of the Supreme God.
;(036)
*'''Question''': What is the Wild Soul (''aramitama'')?
*'''Answer''': ''Ara'' is valor; it is advancement.
;(037)
*'''Question''': What is the Wondrous Soul (''kushimitama'')?
*'''Answer''': ''Kushi'' is wisdom; it is skillfulness.
;(038)
*'''Question''': What is the Fortunate Soul (''sachimitama'')?
*'''Answer''': ''Sachi'' is love; it is benefit.
;(039)
*'''Question''': What is the Harmonious Soul (''nigimitama'')?
*'''Answer''': ''Nigi'' is affinity; it is peace. :Valor, wisdom, love, and affinity are the unchanging Way. :Advancement, skillfulness, benefit, and peace are said to be merely their functions. :For this reason, they respond to time and events, appearing in a hundred ways and a thousand transformations. :One cannot view them with a fixed pattern.
;(040)
*'''Question''': Does "advancement" mean only moving forward?
*'''Answer''': It is to advance—whether by curving, bending, slanting, or going straight—when it is right to advance.
;(041)
*'''Question''': Is the function of Valor merely advancement?
*'''Answer''': It is decisiveness.
;(042)
*'''Question''': Is the function of Wisdom merely skillfulness?
*'''Answer''': It is discernment.
;(043)
*'''Question''': Is the function of Love merely benefit?
*'''Answer''': It is nurturance.
;(044)
*'''Question''': Is the function of Affinity merely peace?
*'''Answer''': It is interaction.
;(045)
*'''Question''': Do Heaven, Earth, and Man share the same Way?
*'''Answer''': It is the same Way, but it is called the Way of Heaven, the Way of Earth, and the Way of Man. :Because their respective forms have differences in size, weight, and importance, their names are different.
;(046)
*'''Question''': Can the Way be controlled by the Four Souls?
*'''Answer''': The Way is such that valor cannot move it, wisdom cannot measure it, love cannot seize it, and affinity cannot steal it.
;(047)
*'''Question''': What is the Great Way [大道, ''Daidō'']?
*'''Answer''': When the Four Souls are united in the Way, the統 (''tō'', unified whole) is called the Great Way.
;(048)
*'''Question''': In human interaction, should one respond with one soul or with the Four Souls?
*'''Answer''': One responds to a ruler with the way of a minister, and to a father with the way of a son. :The rest can be known by this standard. :The Four Souls emerge and recede from each other in response to the time, place, and station, adapting to the circumstances. :Even within a single conversation, in the space of a single sound or phrase, they appear and disappear interchangeably and cannot be fully fathomed. :Therefore, it is said of the Way: one should step back and reflect, and carefully discern, "This phrase was love, that phrase was affinity, this phrase was wisdom, that phrase was valor." Afterwards, one can clarify what within it was in accord with the Way and what was not. :This is called the way of self-reflection.
;(049)
*'''Question''': Is it "God and the Way are eternal" or "The Way and God are eternal"?
*'''Answer''': The Way and God are not two. :The word "and" is an impropriety of human speech. :"Eternal" in contrast to "brief" is an impropriety.
;(050)
*'''Question''': How does one verify the Way?
*'''Answer''': To verify the Way, one must mutually verify the past, present, and future. :One verifies the Way with the Way.
;(051)
*'''Question''': What is the Way?
*'''Answer''': The Way is singular and without mixture.
;(052)
*'''Question''': Liking something belongs to which soul?
*'''Answer''': Liking comes from love.
;(053)
*'''Question''': Does God breathe?
*'''Answer''': God certainly breathes. :Therefore, there is nothing among the ten thousand things that does not breathe.
;(054)
*'''Question''': Is it that the Celestial Emperor exists, and therefore the great atmosphere exists? Or that the great atmosphere exists, and therefore the Celestial Emperor exists?
*'''Answer''': First there is Spirit, and after that there is Power. :First there is Power, and after that there is the great atmosphere. :The great atmosphere that you breathe is not the great atmosphere that the Earth breathes. :The great atmosphere that the Earth breathes is not the great atmosphere that the Sun breathes. :Therefore, know that the great atmosphere that the Celestial Emperor breathes is not the great atmosphere that manifest bodies breathe.
;(055)
*'''Question''': Does the "liking" in "liking valor" also come from love?
*'''Answer''': It also comes from love. :One must surely love something, and for that reason, one likes valor. :It is an excess of love.
;(056)
*'''Question''': Righteousness [義, ''gi''] belongs to which soul?
*'''Answer''': Each of the Four Souls has Righteousness. :It governs judging, regulating, deciding, and dividing. :If we assign them to the Four Souls: judging is Wisdom; regulating is Affinity; deciding is Valor; and dividing is Love. :Judging includes the meaning of mending and patching. :Regulating includes the meaning of government and laws. :Deciding includes the meaning of being resolute and daring. :Dividing includes the meaning of forgetting oneself for a cause.
;(057)
*'''Question''': Is correcting a fault a matter of Righteousness or of the Upright Spirit [直靈, ''naohi'']?
*'''Answer''': Correcting a fault is Righteousness. :The Upright Spirit is what extinguishes a fault before it sprouts. :Each soul has its function, and that which is "upright" within them is the Upright Spirit. :The Upright Spirit is not bound by time or circumstance.
;(058)
*'''Question''': The Upright Spirit seems like the "innate good" of each soul. :What is this innate goodness and innate ability?
*'''Answer''': The Upright Spirit is the name for the most refined and subtle aspect of each soul. :A decision taken from the Upright Spirit is perfectly good and perfectly beautiful. :Therefore, it differs from the meaning of judging, regulating, deciding, and dividing.
;(059)
*'''Question''': Should a ruler die for the altars of state, and a great officer die for the domain?
*'''Answer''': This refers to one who dies while practicing benevolence and treading the path of righteousness. :A tyrannical ruler or a corrupt official cannot claim the name of having died for the altars of state or for the domain.
;(060)
*'''Question''': Those who can perfect their heavenly endowment are few, but can they become gods?
*'''Answer''': A good soul can become a god. :One who dies with true valor is led by the Valor Soul. :One who dies with true wisdom is led by the Wisdom Soul. :One who dies with true love is led by the Love Soul. :One who dies with true affinity is led by the Affinity Soul. :Therefore, even if one's virtue is not perfect, one can surely become a god of one type.
;(061)
*'''Question''': Ōkuninushi is already the complete god of the Earthly Way. :Why are there Four Souls in addition?
*'''Answer''': He has always possessed the Four Souls. :When they are united, he is the god Ōkuninushi.
;(062)
*'''Question''': What is the method for doing good?
*'''Answer''': God has no day or night. :To speak and act for the good is how good is accomplished.
;(063)
*'''Question''': It seems humanity's great desire is for fame. :I do not know if God also has desires.
*'''Answer''': God's great desire is only for goodness. :Humanity's desire for fame is also a good.
;(064)
*'''Question''': Must God wait for a person to ask before He teaches?
*'''Answer''': Because of not knowing, one first asks. :Because of knowing, one then teaches.
;(065)
*'''Question''': When human souls become gods, are there ranks of high and low, superior and inferior?
*'''Answer''': One with complete virtue is of the highest rank. :One with three virtues is of the middle rank. :One with two virtues is of the lower rank. :One with one virtue is of the lowest rank. :One with not a single good deed to their name remains amongst the grass and weeds.
;(066)
*'''Question''': How does one prove goodness?
*'''Answer''': One proves it by the Sun, the Earth, and the Moon.
;(067)
*'''Question''': May I venture to ask the meaning of that?
*'''Answer''': From the beginning of creation, for eternity without limit, they do not dare to lose their Way.
;(068)
*'''Question''': What is the meaning of "regulating includes government and laws"?
*'''Answer''': Government is correctness. :Edicts are principle. :Law is public fairness. :Standards are uniformity.
;(069)
*'''Question''': To what does reverence and caution belong?
*'''Answer''': It belongs to Righteousness.
;(070)
*'''Question''': To what does desire belong?
*'''Answer''': Desire comes from the Four Souls and stands alongside Righteousness.
;(071)
*'''Question''': To what does wishing belong?
*'''Answer''': It belongs to desire.
;(072)
*'''Question''': Desire stands alongside Righteousness, and Righteousness involves judging, regulating, deciding, and dividing. :In that case, what are the objects of desire?
*'''Answer''': Fame, status, longevity, and wealth. :For fame, one desires beauty. :For status, one desires height. :For longevity, one desires length. :For wealth, one desires greatness.
;(073)
*'''Question''': To what does aspiration [志, ''kokorozashi''] belong?
*'''Answer''': It belongs to thought.
;(074)
*'''Question''': Did God create the human ancestor with special intent?
*'''Answer''': Yes. :The reason it is so is that He bestowed the spiritual soul. :This is the proof.
;(075)
*'''Question''': What does the Celestial Emperor take as His soul?
*'''Answer''': Valor, Wisdom, Affinity, and Love.
;(076)
*'''Question''': What does the Celestial Emperor take as His power?
*'''Answer''': Motion, stillness, attraction, repulsion, condensation, dissolution, separation, and union.
;(077)
*'''Question''': What does the Celestial Emperor take as His body?
*'''Answer''': The Hard, the Soft, and the Fluid.
;(078)
*'''Question''': In that case, the Celestial Emperor takes Valor, Wisdom, Affinity, and Love as His heart; Motion, Stillness, etc., as His power; and Fluid, Soft, and Hard as His body, thereby being a God. :I am not sure of His size.
*'''Answer''': He is so great there is nothing outside Him, and so small there is nothing inside Him. :It is as if He is nowhere, yet as if there is nowhere He is not.
;(079)
*'''Question''': Does patience belong to Righteousness?
*'''Answer''': It belongs to thought.
;(080)
*'''Question''': Does diligence also belong to thought?
*'''Answer''': Yes.
;(081)
*'''Question''': Thought belongs to which soul?
*'''Answer''': The thought of Valor is Valor. :The thought of Wisdom is Wisdom. :The thought of Love is Love. :The thought of Affinity is Affinity.
;(082)
*'''Question''': Patience and diligence are human affairs; God should have no trouble with such thoughts. :But does God also possess this thing called "thought"?
*'''Answer''': He thinks what man does not think, is patient where man is not patient, and is diligent where man is not diligent.
;(083)
*'''Question''': What does God endure with patience?
*'''Answer''': He endures the goodness He has received from Heaven.
;(084)
*'''Question''': Do joy, anger, sorrow, and pleasure belong to all Four Souls?
*'''Answer''': All Four Souls possess them.
;(085)
*'''Question''': Do the appetites that arise depending on the body belong to the love of self-love?
*'''Answer''': Yes. :This is the primary meaning of the goodness endowed by Heaven to the human ancestor.
;(086)
*'''Question''': The Celestial Emperor is all-wise and all-powerful, and is the Lord. :Why did He create many gods?
*'''Answer''': The Celestial Emperor created many gods in order to be all-wise and all-powerful. :Having created the great Sun, He endowed it with a spiritual soul, spiritual power, and a spiritual body. :Having created the great Earth, He endowed it with a spiritual soul, spiritual power, and a spiritual body. :Having created the great Moon, He endowed it with a spiritual soul, spiritual power, and a spiritual body. :The great Sun, the great Earth, the great Moon, and the constellations live forever and do not die. :But humankind must die. :Their spirits also become gods. :If the Celestial Emperor did not create many gods, what sort of Lord would He preside over?
;(087)
*'''Question''': Since the Celestial Emperor created humankind, does He also protect them Himself?
*'''Answer''': First is dwelling on Earth, second is dwelling in the Sun, third is dwelling in the Moon. :For ten thousand generations, this is not violated.
;(088)
*'''Question''': Must the human spirit rely on words?
*'''Answer''': Sometimes it relies on words, sometimes it does not.
;(089)
*'''Question''': Is prayer beneficial?
*'''Answer''': Sometimes it is beneficial, sometimes it is not.
;(090)
*'''Question''': I venture to ask about the functions of Wisdom.
*'''Answer''': Feeling, realization, awakening, and discernment.
;(091)
*'''Question''': I venture to ask about the functions of Love.
*'''Answer''': Creating, begetting, transforming, and nurturing.
;(092)
*'''Question''': I venture to ask about the functions of Affinity.
*'''Answer''': Cultivating, purifying, governing, and pacifying.
;(093)
*'''Question''': I venture to ask about the functions of Valor.
*'''Answer''': Striving, exerting, overcoming, and advancing.
;(094)
*'''Question''': Can the four desires for fame, status, longevity, and wealth be matched to the Four Souls?
*'''Answer''': Fame matches with Wisdom. :Status matches with Valor. :Longevity matches with Love. :Wealth matches with Affinity.
;(095)
*'''Question''': Regarding "spirit communing with Spirit," I have heard your teaching. :But I do not know if the communing party distinguishes between self and other.
*'''Answer''': Sometimes, one's own spirit communes with one's own spirit. :Sometimes, one's own spirit communes with the spirit of one other person. :Sometimes, one's own spirit communes with the spirit of a nation. :Sometimes, one's own spirit communes with the spirits of countless millions. :The same is true for body communing with body, and power with power.
;(096)
*'''Question''': Does the soul have a warp and a weft?
*'''Answer''': Valor and Affinity are the warp. :Wisdom and Love are the weft.
;(097)
*'''Question''': What is the meaning of "warp-words"?
*'''Answer''': From one to ten thousand, they are fixed and unchanging. :This is what is meant by "warp-words" [i.e., fundamental principles].
;(098)
*'''Question''': What is the meaning of "weft-words"?
*'''Answer''': To control, manipulate, give, and take—the authority for this rests with me. :This is what is meant by "weft-words" [i.e., adaptive application].
;(099)
*'''Question''': Does the Way have public and private aspects?
*'''Answer''': The Way has large and small aspects, while affairs have public and private aspects. :The Way is a name common to all, from the Son of Heaven down to the common people. :When the Way is established, affairs can be carried out.
;(100)
*'''Question''': Does secrecy belong to the private sphere?
*'''Answer''': Secrecy and the private are different. :Secrecy is a matter of primary concern.
;(101)
*'''Question''': To which soul does the heart of compassion belong?
*'''Answer''': It belongs to Love.
;(102)
*'''Question''': To which soul does the heart of deference and yielding belong?
*'''Answer''': It belongs to the Way. :Birds, beasts, insects, and fish do not have this heart.
;(103)
*'''Question''': To which soul does the heart of shame and dislike of evil belong?
*'''Answer''': All Four Souls possess it. :Beasts and birds feel no shame.
;(104)
*'''Question''': To which soul does the heart of right and wrong belong?
*'''Answer''': All Four Souls have their own right and wrong.
;(105)
*'''Question''': To which soul does admonition and remonstrance belong?
*'''Answer''': All Four Souls possess it. :What beasts and birds contend for is only sex and food.
;(106)
*'''Question''': To which soul does solemnity belong?
*'''Answer''': Solemnity belongs to Affinity. :It speaks of an appearance that has a majesty that cannot be violated.
;(107)
*'''Question''': In that case, do gravity, solemnity, impartiality, and correctness belong to Affinity?
*'''Answer''': They belong to Affinity.
;(108)
*'''Question''': How are loyalty and filial piety considered the Way?
*'''Answer''': Loyalty and filial piety are inherent in the human heart but have not yet taken form. :In a time before rulers and fathers, this heart always existed, hence it is called the Way. :When there is a ruler and a father, the "way of serving" is established. :The "serving" in "serving the ruler" belongs to the Affinity soul. :The Way is established without limit. :Therefore, to speak of the way without the "affair" [of serving] is a matter of human relations.
;(109)
*'''Question''': What of strange divine phenomena?
*'''Answer''': Upon seeing a strange sight, one must be reverent. :Upon hearing of a monstrous thing, one must be cautious.
;(110)
*'''Question''': Does learning belong to Wisdom and teaching to Love?
*'''Answer''': Both belong to all Four Souls.
;(111)
*'''Question''': What is the distinction between Heavenly Gods [天神, ''tenshin''] and Earthly Deities [地祇, ''chigi'']?
*'''Answer''': Those who exist in the formless are called Heavenly Gods. :Those who exist in the formed are called Earthly Deities.
;(112)
*'''Question''': When human souls become gods, do they belong to Heaven or to Earth?
*'''Answer''': Those within the ''zero-sphere'' atmosphere [零圍氣, ''reii-ki''] belong to Earth. :Those outside the ''zero-sphere'' atmosphere belong to Heaven.
;(113)
*'''Question''': What is spiritual authority [靈威, ''rei'i'']?
*'''Answer''': Spiritual authority is such that the spiritual soul moves even things without emotion. :How much more so those with emotion?
;(114)
*'''Question''': Ōkuninushi is the spirit of the great Earth and certainly not a human body. :He has already submitted to the Heavenly Mandate. :I do not understand why it is a mistake to call him a "Child of God" [神子, ''shinko''].
*'''Answer''': What has been called "Child of God" since ancient times is merely the functioning of Ōkuninushi's spirit, power, and body. :The great Earth has no empress or consort, so how could it have a child? *'''Meiji 16 [1883], October.''' *'''The ultimate meaning of pacifying the soul and returning to the divine is finished.'''
==Source== *[https://wikisource.org/wiki/%E7%9C%9E%E9%81%93%E5%95%8F%E5%B0%8D 真道問対] on Wikisource

revision.comment: Text replacement - "[[sn:" to "[[Category:sn_crud|TEST"
revision.text:
